mnemonics.title: SDLC Sustainability Phases
mnemonics.content: Remember SDLC-S: Study environmental impacts → Design for Environment → Limit energy consumption → Consider disposal → Study continuously. Alternatively: Please Review Test Each Design Systematically (Planning, Requirements, Testing, Evaluation, Design, System)

acronyms.usage: Evaluating environmental impacts from raw materials to disposal
Integrating environmental factors in product design
Technology for information processing and communication
Discarded electronic devices requiring special disposal
Standard unit for measuring carbon footprint

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a pie chart: Total Facility Energy is the whole pie, IT Equipment Energy is the computing portion. PUE shows how much 'overhead' energy is wasted on cooling, lighting, etc. The closer to 1.0, the bigger the computing slice.
Picture a figure-8 infinity loop: Raw Materials → Manufacturing → Distribution → Use → Disposal → Recycling feeds back into Raw Materials (cradle-to-cradle concept).
A triangle with Environmental Sustainability at one corner, Economic Viability at another, and Social Responsibility at the third - all three must be balanced.

analogies.analogy: Like a multiplex theater showing multiple movies on one screen, virtualization runs multiple 'virtual computers' on a single physical server, maximizing resource use.
Think of PUE like fuel efficiency in a car - lower numbers mean better efficiency, like miles per gallon. A PUE of 1.0 is like a car using all fuel for movement with zero waste.
LCA is like tracing a food item from farm to table to compost and back - understanding the full environmental journey of a product.
DfE is like building a house with recyclable materials and solar panels from the start, rather than retrofitting later - environmental thinking built into the foundation.
